last_analyst_note:
Mode 2 (human notes) run @ 2026-04-25T17:19:36.077Z. Picked HNOTE-001 §B3 (prefix unification) as the next pending sub-item per the previous run's recommendation. Produced a complete B3 EXP locally — boundary-regex two-pass rename for 65 Phase A1 component classes (337+107+47 + 3 special-case replacements across generate-html.js, sections.json, test.js).

DUPLICATE COLLISION DETECTED at write-back: a parallel analyst run had already produced EXP-251 (B3 prefix unification, completed 16:19:01Z) but my Step 0a clean-clone refresh read a stale tracker (items=248, next_id=250) before the FUSE-side state caught up. I wrote my EXP at slot EXP-250 (collided with the prior B2 scope-obsolete entry's name root and stomped on next_id). Self-cleanup performed:

  • expansion-tracker.json: removed my EXP-250 entry; restored next_id 251→252.
  • HNOTE-001 consumed_progress.B3_prefix_unification: restored to point at EXP-251 (the prior run's canonical delivery).
  • monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-250-phase-b3-prefix-unification.json: overwritten with a withdrawal stub (item_id=EXP-250-phase-b3-WITHDRAWN, status=withdrawn, do_not_integrate=true, superseded_by=EXP-251).
  • monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-EXP-250-phase-b3-ready.json: overwritten with a withdrawal stub (type=withdrawn, do_not_integrate=true).

EXP-251 is the canonical B3 delivery and remains intact. Decider should integrate EXP-249 (B1) and EXP-251 (B3) per their respective proposals; my EXP-250-phase-b3 file should be ignored.

Cross-check on EXP-251 vs my withdrawn EXP: EXP-251 uses a CLEANER design that intentionally does NOT rename verdict-modifier classes (.vb-refuted, .vb-recycled, .vt-misleading, .vl-std, etc.) — they stay as a "verdict token namespace" unprefixed. CSS rules become e.g. .ds-verdict-badge.vb-recycled{...} and HTML emits class="ds-verdict-badge vb-${verdict}". This is internally consistent (CSS and HTML both reference the same unprefixed verdict-modifier) and avoids the special-case template-literal handling my approach required. EXP-251's choice is the better design; adopt as canonical.

Phase B status: B1 delivered (EXP-249, queued for curmudgeon), B2 scope-obsolete (closed prior run), B3 delivered (EXP-251, ready for integration). HNOTE-001 ready to flip to "consumed" once decider integrates EXP-249 and EXP-251.

Operational lesson logged: Step 0a's clean-clone refresh narrows but does not eliminate the stale-tracker race. Two analyst runs interleaving over the same HNOTE sub-item can both pass the "items=N, next_id=M" sanity gate at start and produce conflicting writes. Recommended tinker fix (Phase 3.1 scheduler-side guard or analogue): add a brief lock-file or conditional check that re-reads tracker.next_id from FUSE just before writing the new EXP, and aborts/renumbers if the slot has been claimed since session start.

No code change to dome site monitoring scripts or repo data files. No new issues created. All JSON files written validate.

prev_analyst_run.note:
Mode 2 (human notes) run @ 2026-04-25T16:19:01.613Z. Per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003, picked up HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 §B3 (the last unconsumed Phase B commission item; B1 shipped EXP-249 prior, B2 closed scope-obsolete EXP-250 prior).

EXP-251 (Phase B3 — design-system prefix unification): Inventoried every Phase A1 component class across build-scripts/generate-html.js (1897 lines) + data/sections.json + data/wins.json + test.js. Cataloged 58 component classes to rename and explicit out-of-scope token/modifier sets (vb-/vt-/vl-/v-/tag- verdict tokens; ps-/ks- progressive-disclosure scaffold; .active/.accent/.sc-sm/.sc-hero modifiers — note .sc-sm/.sc-hero ARE renamed because they are sc- family components, not modifiers in the OOCSS sense). Decision: PREPEND ds- (not REPLACE) — operator phrasing implied prefix-prepend; preserves component sub-namespaces; lowest-risk 1:1 rename; resolves the vb-/vb-stdmodel namespace collision (post-rename: ds-vb-bar is structural, vb-stdmodel is verdict token, both unambiguous). Industry precedent: Bootstrap bs-, Tailwind tw-, GitHub Primer prc-.

Deliverables: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-251-phase-b3-prefix-unification.json (~44KB, 30 top-level keys including kernel_of_truth, rationale_for_prepend_not_replace, inventory_summary, rename_table, excluded_tokens_and_rationale, files_affected, rename_script, test_js_patches, acceptance_criteria, verification_steps, decider_integration_recipe, out_of_scope_explicit, scope_creep_flags, notes_for_decider, notes_for_human_review_group). Inline rename_script.contents is 10000-byte node script using a 4-pass context-aware approach (HTML class= attrs, JSON-escaped class= attrs, CSS selectors with terminator lookahead, DOM API patterns); verifier uses class-name-aware boundary lookbehind/lookahead (NOT \b — \btab-content\b falsely matches inside ds-tab-content because - is non-word). 7-row test_js_patches table covers regex literals and string fragments the auto-rename cannot safely rewrite.

Smoke-test (in /tmp): 516 valid replacements (406 generate-html.js + 71 sections.json + 1 wins.json + 38 test.js), zero false positives (the previously-broken `const evidence = ...` JS variable is correctly preserved), 2 expected residuals in test.js regex literals (covered by TEST_JS_PATCHES). Post-rename: node --check passes for both .js files; JSON.parse passes for both .json files. node build-scripts/generate-html.js produces valid docs/index.html (1317.2 KB vs 1311.8 KB pristine — +5.4 KB consistent with class-prefix expansion); spot-check of diff confirms every change is a class= or .X CSS selector rename, no spurious deltas. Sample post-rename classes in HTML: ds-breaking-news, ds-bn-item, ds-bn-date, ds-ca-tag tag-false (token preserved), ds-sc-card ds-sc-sm (modifier renamed), ds-scorecard ds-sc-domains, ds-vb-bar ds-vb-glyph, ds-verdict-bars, ds-verdict-bar-row, ds-verdict-legend, ds-skip-link, ds-stance-statement, ds-tab-content, ds-tab-btn, ds-toc, ds-pie-slice ds-pie-label ds-pie-callout. Output: 908 ds- prefixed classes in rendered HTML.

Completion-signal proposal: monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-EXP-251-phase-b3-ready.json. Tracker advanced: items=250, next_id=252. HNOTE-001 consumed_progress now records all three commission items (B1 delivered, B2 scope-obsolete, B3 delivered); partial_progress_note updated to flag HNOTE-001 ready for status=consumed flip.

Decider next steps: (1) push EXP-251 to curmudgeon priority queue under DIRECTIVE-003 design rubric; (2) hold integration until curmudgeon clears; (3) auto-integrate after clear (no operator approval gate per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003); (4) when integrating, use the rename_script.contents and apply TEST_JS_PATCHES manually. Conflict-area: EXP-211-redesign-v2 (DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW) touches sc-card sublabel — sequencing notes flagged in EXP-251 notes_for_decider. After all three Phase B EXPs integrate, HNOTE-PHASE-B-VERDICT-CHIP-DEFINITION-LINK-002 (lowest-priority within Phase B) becomes the next Mode 2 target per drain priority.
